CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-08-2008 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-10-2008 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: DOW PATTEN ESQUIRE (LAW OFFICES OF WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY, 415.675.7705) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LISA BERKOWITZ, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 554.3825) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff fails to present evidence sufficient to establish a prima facie case for plaintiff has not shown that the City is a joint employer under Vernon v. State of California (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 114. Assuming plaintiff did shift the burden of proof, defendant has established a nondiscriminatory reason for its decision. Although plaintiff presents evidence that he is a member of more than one protected class, he presents no evidence of disperate treatment. The statement attributed to Sgt. Tom is a stray comment as a matter of law. The conduct established by plaintiff does not support a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Finally, the decision to appoint plaintiff is a discretionary decision by the Chief of Police and it appears that Government Code section 818.4 grants the Chief, and in turn the City, immunity from suit when making that decision. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied for failure to provide the requisite showing under CCP 437c and for failing to explain why plaintiff has not had an adequate opportunity to obtain the evidence. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER AND JUDGMENT. (302/PJM/301-AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
